name: Mnestis Gear.
rule: The toughest Mnestis Battles will award you special Mnestis Gear\!  These are the most powerful items in the game, and, as such, these are restricted to the Mnestis Theatre; you cannot use these in normal campaign Battles.

name: Nymphs.
rule: Some Mnestis Battles will unlock new Nymph Summonings for use in your campaigns. These Nymphs may have been attainable during normal play, but they were missable.

name: Skyseer Titan Expansion.
rule:
When you win the appropriate Mnestis Battle, if you have the Skyseer Expansion, read the following paragraph:

   “You enter the Mnestis Theatre and the machina does the rest, conjuring up scenes most fantastic. You inhabit your roles and the play starts. Everything goes the way it always does: you battle the dreamt-up Primordial, an impossible challenge but a marginal risk, a way to hone your skills and discover new tactics. As the monster falls to its imaginary death, something unexpected  happens. The Mnestis lights dim and go out, and the scene dissipates before your very eyes. you are back in the Theatre chamber,  for you never truly left. It was all shadowplay.  And yet, amongst you, a fifth Titan now stands, one you do not recognize, but that clearly dons an Antikratos Helm. Their giant fleshy  wings spread wide, like a shroud. Their coarse skin has endured much hardship, and is both sunburned and windswept. An inscription on the helm reads: child of the Icarcore, the seer not of time, but of place. But what does that mean?”



   Gain 1 Skyseer Titan and the Skyreach and Horizon’s Edge cards (Secret deck 12, cards 179 and 180). All of these can be used outside of the Mnestis Theatre as well.



   1. **Skyseer Technology.** The Skyseer Technology describes the way you can create more Skyseer class Titans. This Technology can be used during any of your campaigns (meaning the Skyseer Titan is not restricted to the Mnestis Theatre).

   1. **Skyseer Breeding.** Lose 2 Dreamwalker Titans to note the Skyseer Event on the Timeline, 20 days from now. When you resolve this event, gain a Skyseer Titan. You cannot use this ability again until you resolve this event.

   2. **Instant Drop.** Lose 1 Ascended Core and 1 Dreamwalker Titan to immediately gain a Skyseer Titan.

name: Abysswalk.
rule: If the Primordial moves away from you because of a Response, move your Titan to become adjacent.

name: Acrobatics.
rule: When you would receive Knockdown while adjacent to the Primordial, resolve Vault instead.

name: Airburst X.
rule: Titans in Zone X suffer Knockdown and Knockback 1\.

name: Ambrosia Limit X/+X.
rule: Your Ambrosia Limit becomes X or is increased by X, respectively. If you have more than one instance of the former, then the one with the greatest X is used (before applying modifiers). Ambrosia Limit X does not stack, Ambrosia Limit \+X does.

name: Armor-Piercing.
rule: During the Power Roll, ignore Hardened.

name: Armor Reroll X.
rule: During the Armor Roll step of a Primordial Attack Sequence, you may reroll up to X Armor dice.

name: Ascended.
rule: This Gear ignores Power Level penalties.

name: Assist X.
rule: During another Titan’s Power Roll, if, after applying all bonuses, their total Power is less than the AT value and you are within your weapon’s range, you may gain 1 Rage and a Combat Fatigue to immediately add X Break tokens to the Kratos Pool. This ability can only be used if it allows the Attacker to Wound.

name: Attack Reroll X.
rule: During an Attack Roll, you may re-roll up to X Attack dice with no cost.

name: Auto-Black X.
rule: During the first ability window, place X Black tokens ([X.1.9.6](#bookmark=id.p3ne2sxrowif)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Auto-Break X.
rule: During the first ability window, place X Break tokens ([X.1.9.1](#bookmark=id.r3x6kqp0h983)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Auto-Hope X.
rule: During the first ability window, place X Hope tokens ([X.1.9.7](#bookmark=id.3clflhdrzgnu)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Auto-Inspire.
rule: You may stand up as a free action. You may use this ability while Knocked Down.

name: Auto-Rouse X.
rule: During the first ability window, place X Rouse tokens ([X.1.9.6](#bookmark=id.j94s92mznx4g)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Awakening Lock.
rule: You cannot Awaken.

name: Black X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Black tokens ([X.1.9.6](#bookmark=id.p3ne2sxrowif)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Bleeding X.
rule: Gain the *Bleeding* Condition card (unless you already have it) and place X Bleeding tokens on it.

name: Bleeding Limit X/+X.
rule: Your Bleeding Limit becomes X or is increased by X, respectively. If you have more than one instance of the former, then the one with the greatest X is used (before applying modifiers). Bleeding Limit X does not stack, Bleeding Limit \+X does.

name: Block X.
rule: After counting hits from an Evasion Roll, subtract X hits.

name: Break X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Break tokens ([X.1.9.1](#bookmark=id.r3x6kqp0h983)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Burden X.
rule: During the First or second ability window, take X Condition, Despair, or Ambrosia tokens from the nearest Titan. *(This does not count as gaining those tokens.)*

name: Burn.
rule:
Gain 1 Danger for each *Metal* Gear card you have equipped and discard all *Flammable* Gear cards. If this is part of a Primordial Attack Sequence, add the Danger gained to the Danger gained from hits.

   1. **Cycle V.** In Cycle V, when you resolve Burn, first discard 1 Aether token, then proceed with the standard effects.

name: Bypass.
rule:
You may move through other Titans. You cannot end your voluntary movement on a space occupied by another Titan.

   1. **Primordial Bypass.** You can move through, but not end on, the Primordial.

name: Carving X.
rule: When you Wound the Primordial, test Wisdom (8+). On a success, you immediately gain X additional non-Core Primordial resources from that BP card. These resources are not multiplied by the Primordial level.

name: Closing X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Closing tokens ([X.1.9.8](#bookmark=id.adofg35e9zes)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Clutch.
rule: During the Power Roll step, if, after applying bonuses, you have less total Power than the AT value by exactly 1, you may gain 1 Power. After you finish resolving this Attack Sequence, discard your active weapon.

name: Combo-Breaker: X spaces.
rule:
When you would break a Combo, you may move X spaces before checking range. You may only use this ability if it would allow you to break a Combo. This ability is not stackable.

   1. **Self Combo-Breaker: X spaces.** Identical to Combo-Breaker, but you can only use the Gear card which this ability is on to break the Combo (you do not have to exhaust an additional card). This Gear must still possess a matching Weapon Trait.

name: Commit (X).
rule:
This Keyword forces a cost to pay in order to declare this Weapon as an Active Weapon ([X.5.1](#bookmark=id.1rcns5d3xa6j)). If…

   1. …no parentheses, you must gain 1 Fate to declare this weapon as your active weapon.

   2. …X is a negative token, you must gain 1 of that token type to declare this weapon as your active weapon.

   3. …X is a positive token, you must discard 1 of that  token type to declare this weapon as your active weapon.

   4. …X is a Charge token, you must discard 1 Charge token to declare this weapon as your active weapon.

name: Consume.
rule: Treat “consume” as an Instant Death effect.

name: Crash.
rule: Gain 1 Danger and Knockdown.

name: Cryptex Loathing.
rule: Treat your Cryptex gear card as blank.

name: Cumbersome.
rule: During the second ability window, if you successfully wounded with this weapon, exhaust it. This is treated as a voluntary exhaust.

name: Cursed.
rule: During your Titan Attack, you cannot re-roll d10s.

name: Dare.
rule: When you are about to draw an Obol, you may choose to resolve the opposite Obol than the one you draw.

name: Daze.
rule: During either the first or second ability window, if you are adjacent to the Primordial, you may turn the Primordial so that you are in a Rear space.

name: Deadly.
rule: During an Attack Roll, replace one of your regular Attack dice with an additional Crit die. If you roll only one Attack die, you gain a Crit Chance on a natural 9–10 result instead.

name: Death X.
rule: Gain the *Death* Condition card (unless you already have it) and place X Condition tokens on it. If you already have the *Death* Condition card and X is lower than the number of Condition tokens you have, discard Condition tokens until you X matches.

name: Defy X.
rule: While performing a Skill test caused by a Judgement, treat X successful hits as evaded.

name: Deny.
rule: When the Primordial is about to Vanish, Lightbend, or Flicker while within your Weapon’s effective range, ignore that effect and perform an Attack. At the end of the Attack, gain a Combat Fatigue token and draw an Obol.

name: Displace.
rule: Move to an empty space adjacent to its current position. If a Titan is displaced and there is no space it can be legally displaced to, it dies instead.

name: Diversion.
rule: During the second ability window, place a Diversion token ([X.1.9.3](#bookmark=id.8lyfjw1heqy0)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Dodge.
rule: When you are about to resolve an Evasion Roll, gain \+1 Evasion for this roll.

name: Doomed.
rule: During this effect, dice cannot be re-rolled using Fate.

name: Double Commit.
rule: You must gain 2 Fate to declare this weapon as your active weapon.

name: Elation X.
rule: During the second ability window, you may discard X Despair tokens or Ambrosia tokens to place X Opening tokens in the Kratos Pool ([X.1.9.2](#bookmark=id.k0si7ggf4xwr)).

name: Escalate X.
rule: Escalate X times ([X.5.10.6](#bookmark=id.w9s6nftz1xml)). Do not look at the removed cards.

name: Evasion Spiral X.
rule: You may reroll up to X successful evasion dice to lose 1 Fate for each rerolled. If you re-roll a Crit die, you only resolve Crit Evade effects if you roll another ‘10’.

name: Evolving.
rule: When you equip a Gear card with this keyword, write the Gear’s name on your Argonaut sheet in the Mnemos section. The Gear instructs you on what you need to do to evolve it. Track your progress on doing so by marking nodes on the Mnemos track. If this Gear card is equipped by another Argonaut, continue keeping track on the original sheet. If the Argonaut the original sheet belongs to dies, transfer the progress to another living Argonaut’s sheet. If you permanently lose the Gear card, erase it from the sheet; you lose all evolution progress on it.

name: Fate Armor.
rule: This card’s Armor Dice may also be used to mitigate Fate dealt by Primordial Attacks. If you are dealt Fate and Danger, you may distribute the mitigation between the two.

name: Fearless.
rule: Immune to *Fear* and *Dread*.

name: Fire X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Fire tokens ([X.1.9.4](#bookmark=id.x0oyyy5gvmnz)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Float.
rule: Gain the *Float* Condition card, *Float (up)* side up, and place your miniature on its side.

name: Frontlines.
rule: If your Titan is dead, at the End of Battle roll a d10. On a ‘1’, your Argonaut dies as well.

name: Glaciate.
rule:
To Glaciate a space, place a *Black Iceberg* Terrain tile there.

   1. **Giant Glaciate.** To Giant Glaciate a space, place a *Giant Black Iceberg* Terrain tile there.

name: Greater Pass X.
rule: During the Clear the Kratos Pool step, choose up to X Kratos tokens to remain in the pool.

name: Hardened.
rule: A BP card with this keyword has \+2 AT. Ignore this ability if you have a Crit Chance.

name: Harpooned.
rule: During a Titan Attack, if you have at least one hit and become adjacent to the Primordial, immediately move to the VP. Moving outside your effect range doesn’t cause a chain break.

name: Heal.
rule: Take the top card from the Wound Stack and remove it from the Battle. It does not provide resources at the end of the Battle.

name: Heartseeker.
rule: During the Draw BP card step of a Titan Attack Sequence ([X.5.6](#bookmark=id.nej2a87jdn3j)), you may look at the top two BP cards and choose which one to attack. Shuffle the other card back into the deck.

name: Hermes Move X.
rule:
Perform up to X Hermes Moves.

   1. **Hermes Move.** To perform a Hermes Move, draw a straight line in any cardinal direction from the Titan performing the action, stopping when you hit a Terrain tile. Then, choose an Elevated Terrain tile space, either the one the line ended on or one on a space adjacent to the spaces the drawn line went through, and place the Titan on that Terrain tile.

   1. **Must Be Empty.** The chosen Terrain tile space must be empty. When performing more than one Hermes Move from a single action, only the final Hermes Move needs to follow this rule.

name: Hermes Reflex.
rule:
Perform one Hermes Move.

   1. **Advanced Hermes Reflex.** Perform up to two Hermes Moves instead.

name: Hermes Resposition X.
rule: During the second ability window, you may resolve Hermes Move X.

name: Hide.
rule:
If you are not in front of the Primordial, you may activate this ability at the end of your turn to become Hidden. If you have the Priority Target token, pass it to the Titan with the highest Rage other than you.

   1. **Hidden.** As long as you are Hidden, you have \+1 Evasion and \+1 Precision. You stop being Hidden if you move in front of the Primordial, gain the Priority Target token, after the second ability window of your Attack, or at the end of your next turn. *(Place a generic token on the game element granting you Hide as a reminder.)*

name: Hope X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Hope tokens ([X.1.9.7](#bookmark=id.3clflhdrzgnu)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Incinerated.
rule: Treat “incinerated” as an Instant Death effect.

name: Inkblot.
rule: Place an Inkblot Terrain tile under the Primordial.

name: Inspire X.
rule: During the first ability window, choose up to X Knocked Down Titans, they may immediately stand up.

name: Jump.
rule:
At the start of movement, you may activate this ability to ignore up to 1 space of a Terrain tile.

   1. **Advanced Jump.** Ignore up to 2 spaces instead.

name: Knockback X.
rule:
Move the affected miniature X spaces directly away from the source of Knockback in a straight line (or in a specific direction if specified).

   1. **From Attacks.** If Knockback is caused by a Primordial Attack, it affects the Target(s) only if the Target(s) are within the Attack’s range.

   2. **From Responses.** If Knockback is caused by a Primordial Response, it affects the Attacker only if they are within adjacency.

   3. **Knocked Back Titans.**

      1. **Ignore Terrain except Obstacles.** Knocked back Titans ignore all Terrain tiles they move through except Obstacles.

      2. **Destructible Obstacles.** If a Titan would be forced to move through a Destructible Obstacle Terrain tile, stop its movement, remove the Terrain tile from the Battle Board and place the Titan in its space, then the Titan suffers CRASH.

      3. **Indestructible Obstacles.** If a Titan would be forced to move through an Indestructible Obstacle, it stops on an adjacent space right before the Obstacle instead, then suffers CRASH.

   4. **Knocked Back Primordials.** Knocked Back Primordials can move through any Terrain tiles and miniatures without hindrance. They destroy any Terrain tiles (except Indestructible) they move through and cause CRASH and Unavoidable Knockback to Titans as normal.

   5. **Board Edge.** If the affected miniature is Knocked Back into a Board Edge, it continues to move along the edge, if possible, away from the source of Knockback.

name: Knockdown.
rule:
Gain the Knockdown Condition card, “Falling down” side up, and tip your miniature on its side. Follow the rules of the Condition card. *(Note: the definition of Knockdown used in the Learn to Play is incorrect)*

   1. **From Attacks.** If Knockdown is caused by an Attack, it affects the Target only if it is within the Attack’s range.

   2. **From Responses.** If Knockdown is caused by a Response, it affects the Attacker only if they are adjacent.

name: Laser Resistance X.
rule: Each Laser Attack hit deals X less Danger to you. In Cycle IV+, this loses its Keyword status and becomes a Defensive Statistic instead ([V.2.2.4](#bookmark=id.jatfxs92idui)).

name: Lifeline.
rule: When you are about to die from a Chasm space, Displace instead (in Cycle 4, you may instead place the Titan on the closest Irem Tower Terrain tile). Alternatively, when you are about to die from falling from a Boundless Board Edge, stop adjacent to it. This ability can be used even if Knocked Down or otherwise prohibited from using active abilities.

name: Light X.
rule: Game elements on the Battle Board with this keyword emit light and are considered light sources. This ability is disabled on a game element owned by a Titan if that Titan cannot use abilities, the element is exhausted, or the element is Smothered (X.X.X). A game element is within ‘range of light’ if it is within X spaces of a light source. When counting from a Titan light source, only spaces in front of that Titan can be counted (see Titan Facing, X.X.X).

name: Lightbend.
rule: If possible, place a Petrified Vent Terrain tile centered under the Primordial and the Primordial disappears.

name: Liquid Edge.
rule: When checking your weapon’s effect range, you may count along diagonal spaces.

name: Lumbering.
rule: Cannot be involuntarily moved.

name: Masterwork.
rule: During an Attack Roll, you may reroll 1s with no cost.

name: Midas X.
rule: Place X Midas tokens ([X.7.16](#bookmark=id.7dxryujxokoz)) on your gear cards.

name: Midas Immune.
rule: Ignore the effects of Midas tokens placed on this particular gear card.

name: Motivate X.
rule: During the second ability window, choose another Titan. That Titan may move up to X spaces. This ability is not required to Stack ([I.3.6.1](#bookmark=id.dqwxdwx561lr)).

name: Opening X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Opening tokens in the Kratos Pool ([X.1.9.2](#bookmark=id.k0si7ggf4xwr)).

name: Overbreak X.
rule:
1. **Umbral.** (Cycles 1-3 only) During the second ability window, if your Total Power exceeds the AT value by at least 1, place X Break tokens in the Kratos Pool ([X.1.9.1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.r3x6kqp0h983)).

   2. **Illuminated.** (Cycles 4-5 only) Break X. Then, if your Total Power exceeds the AT value by at least 1, place up to X additional Break tokens but no more than the exceeded value.

name: Pain X.
rule: Gain X Pain tokens.

name: Pass X.
rule: During the Clear the Kratos Pool step, choose up to X Opening and/or Break tokens to remain in the Kratos Pool.

name: Perishable.
rule: After you finish resolving a Titan Attack Sequence with this weapon, discard it. *(If the sequence is interrupted, it is still considered resolved.)*

name: Pole Position.
rule: During either the First or second ability window, if you’re adjacent to the Primordial, you may place your Titan on any other empty space adjacent to the Primordial.

name: Power Reroll X.
rule: During the Power Roll step of an Attack, you may re-roll up to X Power dice.

name: Precise.
rule: During the Draw a BP card step ([X.5.6](#bookmark=id.nej2a87jdn3j)), after drawing the card you may choose to draw the top card of the BP discard instead. If you do, place the first drawn card back into the BP deck and shuffle it.

name: Provoke.
rule: During the first ability window, gain the Priority Target token and turn the Primordial to face you.

name: Pull X.
rule:
Move the affected miniature X spaces towards the source of the Pull, along the shortest possible path.

   1. **Other miniatures.** The affected miniature can move through spaces occupied by other miniatures.

   2. **Terrain.** The affected miniature must resolve the effects of any Terrain tiles it moves through.

   3. **Destructible Obstacles.** If a Titan is about to be pulled into a Destructible Obstacle (e.g. a Column), remove the Terrain tile from the Battle Board, the Titan suffers CRASH, then continue the Pull.

   4. **Indestructible Obstacles.** If a Titan is about to be pulled into an Indestructible Obstacle, it suffers CRASH and continues to move along the Obstacle’s edge along the shortest possible path towards the Primordial.

   5. **Adjacency.** If the affected miniature is or becomes adjacent to the source, the Pull does not end. Instead, move the source directly away from the affected miniature the remaining number of spaces, then finish the Pull. The source’s movement is voluntary, the affected miniature’s movement is involuntary.

   6. **Titan Timing.** Titans may use the Pull X keyword during either the first or second ability window.

name: Pursuit X.
rule: When resolving an AI card, if you’re not a Target, move X spaces in a straight line in the same direction as the Primordial.

name: Pushback X.
rule:
Turn the source towards the affected miniature. Then, move X spaces in the direction of the affected miniature, pushing it in a straight line.

   1. **Other miniatures.** Pushed Titans can move through spaces occupied by other miniatures. If a Titan ends involuntary movement on a space with another miniature, the other miniature is Displaced.

   2. **Terrain.** The affected miniature must resolve the effects of any Terrain tiles it moves through.

   3. **Shortest path.** If the affected miniature is not adjacent, move the source in its direction along the shortest possible path.

   4. **Destructible Obstacles.** If a Titan is about to be pushed into a Destructible Obstacle, remove that Terrain tile from the Battle Board, the Titan suffers CRASH, then continue Pushback.

   5. **Indestructible Obstacles.** If a Titan is about to be pushed into an Indestructible Obstacle, displace it to an adjacent space that would allow the Primordial to continue movement onto a space previously occupied by the Titan, then continue Pushback. If the Titan dies from displacement, then once the Primordial has moved into the space formerly occupied by the Titan, the Pushback stops.

   6. **Board Edge.** If a Titan or Primordial is about to be pushed into the Board Edge, it is displaced to an adjacent space in a way that would allow the source of Pushback to move onto a space previously occupied by the affected miniature, then continue Pushback.

   7. **Titan Timing.** Titans may resolve the Pushback X keyword during either the first or second ability window of their Attack or when specifically instructed to resolve it immediately.

name: Quantum.
rule: For the purposes of Rapid Adaptation (exclusive to Icarian Harpy), if attacking from the Blindspot, your weapon can have any Weapon Trait.

name: Ranged Y–X.
rule: To attack with this weapon, you must be at least Y and up to X spaces away from your target. This keyword is not stackable.

name: Reach X.
rule: You may attack from up to X spaces away. This keyword is not stackable.

name: Reduction X.
rule:
You may reduce involuntary movement suffered by up to X.

   1. **Wish Away Reduction X.** You may reduce Wish Aways suffered by up to X.

   2. **Pushback Reduction X.** You may reduce Pushbacks suffered by up to X.

   3. **Knockback Reduction X.** You may reduce Knockbacks suffered by up to X.

   4. **Kickback Reduction X.** You may reduce Kickbacks suffered by up to X.

   5. **Pull Reduction X.** You may reduce Pulls suffered by up to X.

name: Reflex.
rule:
Move up to 1 space.

   1. **Advanced Reflex.** 2 spaces instead.

   2. **Superior Reflex.** 3 spaces instead.

name: Reinforce X.
rule:
When you are about to resolve an Armor Roll, add X red dice to your Armor dice pool.

   1. **Advanced Reinforce X.** Add X black dice instead.

   2. **Superior Reinforce X.** Add X white dice instead.

   3. **Mortal Reinforce X.** Add X mortal dice instead.

name: Remote Scan.
rule: When you perform a Scan, you may do so from up to 3 spaces away.

name: Reposition X.
rule: During the second ability window, you may move up to X spaces.

name: Rescan.
rule: When you perform a Scan, you may redraw the Major Trauma you just drew.

name: Restricted (Trait).
rule: You can equip only one gear card with the list Trait.

name: Retreat X.
rule: The Primordial moves X spaces directly away from the Target or the Attacker without turning. If the Target/Attacker is on a VP, choose the direction.

name: Rewind.
rule: Place your Titan on your Time Anchor. If your Time Anchor is not on the Board, you are erased from existence instead.

name: Rocksteady.
rule: When you are about to suffer Knockdown, you may use this ability to ignore it.

name: Rollout.
rule: When you are about to suffer Knockdown while adjacent to the Primordial, suffer Knockback 4 instead.

name: Rouse X.
rule: During the second ability window, place X Rouse tokens ([X.1.9.6](#bookmark=id.j94s92mznx4g)) in the Kratos Pool.

name: Rush.
rule:
Move with \+1 Speed and perform a Melee/Reach Attack with Auto-break 1\. You must move at least 3 spaces and reach your target via the shortest possible path.

   1. **Improved Rush.** \+2 Speed instead and additionally gain \+1 Precision on the Attack.

name: Sacrifice.
rule: During the first ability window, you may gain 1 Danger to place one Break token in the Kratos Pool ([X.1.9.1](#bookmark=id.r3x6kqp0h983)).

name: Scale.
rule:
When you are about to perform a test to climb a Vantage Point or Elevated Terrain, automatically succeed at that test instead.

   1. **Scale X.** May be treated as Scale. Otherwise, during the first or second ability window, if you are adjacent to the Primordial, you may immediately climb a VP, move to another VP, or move X spaces up on the Endless Staircase.

name: Second Chance.
rule: After you draw a Trauma or Obol card, you may ignore its effect and discard it. Draw and resolve another card from the same deck *(note: a discarded Obol is immediately shuffled back into the Obol deck)*. You cannot ignore the second draw in any way. This ability can be used even when knocked down or when an effect says that you cannot use any active abilities.

name: Shaded.
rule: Until the start of the next Titan Round, treat your Titan as being on as shaded space.

name: Skitter.
rule: The Primordial immediately moves X spaces in a straight line in a random direction, where X is its current level.

name: Solace.
rule: Discard a Mind Condition card or a Despair token from yourself or an adjacent Titan.

name: Spiral X.
rule: During the Attack Roll step, you may reroll up to X hits to lose 1 Fate for each. If you reroll a Crit die this way, the original result does not grant you a Crit Chance.

name: Spotlight.
rule: During the first ability window, if you have the Priority Target token, gain 1 Red Power Die for this Attack.

name: Stalwart.
rule: When you are about to suffer Knockback, you may use this ability to ignore it.

name: Startup X.
rule: When you are about to Attack, if there are no Kratos tokens in the Kratos Pool, place X Opening and/or Break tokens in the Kratos Pool.

name: Strikeback X.
rule: If you fully evade a Primordial Attack, you may immediately move up to X spaces and perform a Combat Action, then gain a Combat Fatigue. This is an Interrupt Attack. If this Ability is used during the Primordial Round, gain 1 Power for the Attack.

name: Stuck.
rule: When you initiate an attack with this Weapon, you cannot perform any more voluntary movements this turn.

name: Succor.
rule: During another Titan’s Power Roll, if, after applying all bonuses, they have less total Power than the AT value by exactly 1 and you are within your weapon range of the Primordial, you may gain 1 Rage, a Combat Fatigue, and discard this weapon to add 1 Power to that Power Roll.

name: Super Smart Delivery.
rule: A Titan with sufficient open hand slots may, as a Free Action or a WoO, equip a gear card with Super Smart Delivery from the Armory and gain 1 Rage.

name: Superior Chance.
rule: Second Chance, but if drawing from Obols, first remove the *You died horribly* card from that deck for this draw only.

name: Suppress.
rule:
When you deal a wound[5](#bookmark=kix.cfsnnm125tv2) (before responses)[7](#bookmark=kix.ncneqmlyd9ka), shuffle the discard pile denoted by the subtype into the appropriate deck. You may only use this ability if there is at least one card in the corresponding discard pile[10](#bookmark=kix.cpbzikkpbx61). *(This keyword does not exist without a subtype.)*

   1. **BP Suppress.** Shuffle the BP discard pile into the BP deck.

   2. **AI Suppress.** Shuffle the AI discard pile into the AI deck.

name: Taint X.
rule: During the First Ability Window, you must turn X Break tokens in the Kratos Pool into Black tokens.

name: Temporal  Reflex.
rule: When you are about to suffer CRASH, ignore it. If you would suffer Unavoidable Knockback, displace instead.

name: Tilt.
rule: When you are about to draw a BP card during an Attack, first discard the top BP card.

name: Time-Clocked.
rule: Gain the *Time-Clocked* Condition card, *Departing* side up and remove your miniature from the board. Follow the rules of the Condition card.

name: Tireless.
rule: When you are about to activate the Cumbersome keyword, you may ignore it.

name: Titan Possession.
rule:
Once you equip this Gear card on a Titan, you have to equip it on this Titan for every subsequent Battle. You may, however, junction this Titan with a different Argonaut.

   1. **Argonaut Possession.** Once you equip this Gear card on an Argonaut, you have to equip it on that Argonaut’s Titan for every subsequent Battle.

name: Transform.
rule: Use this ability to flip this Gear card to the other side. This ability can only be used as a free action.

name: Trick.
rule:
Weapons with the Transform keyword and Trick may immediately flip to the other side when you meet the Trick condition. *(This ignores the free action restriction of the Transform keyword.)*

   1. **Trauma Trick.** You may immediately flip this card after you resolve a Trauma card.

   2. **Wound Trick.** You may immediately flip this card after you Wound.

   3. **Fail Trick.** You may immediately flip this card after you Fail to Wound.

name: Truth X.
rule: During the Second Ability Window, place X Break, Opening, Fire, Black, and/or Closing tokens in the Kratos Pool.

name: Tumble.
rule: When you are about to suffer CRASH, roll a d10. On a 6+ ignore it.

name: Unburden X.
rule: During the first or second ability window, give X Condition, Despair, or Ambrosia tokens to the closest Titan. *(This does not count as gaining those tokens.)*

name: Unholy Alchemy.
rule:
Turn all of your Midas tokens into Ambrosia tokens. *(This does not count as gaining or discarding either tokens.)*

   1. **Reverse Unholy Alchemy.** Turn all of your Ambrosia tokens into Midas tokens. *(This does not count as gaining or discarding either tokens.)*

name: Unique.
rule: There can be no more than one instance of a unique card in play or in the Argo Armory at any given time.

name: Unwieldy.
rule: When checking range with this weapon, you can only count spaces in straight lines from you.

name: Ur-Knockback X.
rule: Knockback X towards the closest short Board Edge. This is considered a regular Knockback.

name: Ur-Pull X.
rule: Pull X towards the Ur-Fleece. This is considered a regular Pull.

name: Vault.
rule:
If you are adjacent to the Primordial, place your miniature on an empty space in a straight line from you, on the opposite side of the Primordial, adjacent to it. A Titan may use this ability in either the first or second ability window.

   1. **Forced Vault.** Vault, but treat it as involuntary movement. If you would have to end this movement outside the Board Edge or on an Indestructible Obstacle Terrain tile, you die instead.

   2. **Terrain Vault.** Vault, but check against an adjacent Terrain tile instead of the Primordial.

name: Voluntary Knockdown.
rule: You may gain the *Knockdown (standing up)* Condition card as a free action.

name: Wish Armor.
rule: When you resolve a Wish Attack, you may perform Evasion Rolls and Armor Rolls as if it were a non-Wish Attack. If you exhausted or discarded the gear card that gave you this keyword, you may still use the defensive statistics of that card for this Attack.

name: Wish Away X.
rule: Place the affected Titan on an Irem Tower Terrain tile in front of the Primordial at least X spaces away. If there is no such space, the Titan dies.

name: Wishcursed.
rule: When you trigger Wish Armor, gain 1 Fate.

name: Wish Dodge.
rule:
When you are about to resolve an Evasion Roll, gain \+1 Evasion bonus for this roll if you used Wish Armor.

   1. **Advanced Wish Dodge.** \+2 Evasion bonus instead.

name: Wishrod.
rule: During Targeting, if the Attack is a Wish Attack, you may become the Target instead. The Attack gains Doomed.

name: Siren Survivor.
rule: Remember Ambrosia and Despair are not condition tokens, and that knockdown disallows you from using active abilities. Thus, this Mnemos card’s level 1 ability is useless in Cycles 1 and 2\.

name: Sowed (Secret Card).
rule: 1. **Fated Mnemos Limit**[1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.kk1tk0y01a5n). When gaining this card, ignore the Fated Mnemos limit ([III.6.2](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.6wo45sca918m)).

name: Logicbreaker.
rule: 1. **Logicbreak**[1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.kk1tk0y01a5n)**.** The Combat Action gained from the Logicbreak ability can be used for anything a Combat Action can be used for, but must be used immediately upon gaining. If used to perform a Titan Attack, first Chain Break ([X.7.6](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.yx8r43h369el)).

name: Returner.
rule: 1. **Cheat Fate.** If the “You Live\!” Obol has been removed from the Obol deck, you may still draw a “You Live\!” Obol with the Returner’s Cheat Fate ability.

name: Shadowdancer.
rule:
1. **Shadow Dance.**

   1. **Timing.** The Shadowdance ability can only be activated after fully resolving a Primordial Attack that Targeted the Shadowdancer Titan. If the Attack was not fully resolved (e.g. via Chain Break), you cannot activate this ability.

2. **Combat Action**[13](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.hf4dbt3p0sq9)**.** The Shadowdance ability is supposed to give a Combat Action, not an Attack.

name: Cirrotrireme.
rule: 1. **Shifting Keyword**[14](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.vncfldnua34g)**.** The Shifting keyword on the Cirrotrireme Trireme Argo Ability is an error, ignore it.

name: Pursuer Problem.
rule: 1. **Nemesis and Titan X**[13](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.hf4dbt3p0sq9)**.** The Pursuer Protocol ability can be used to reduce both Nemesis movement and Titan X token movement on the Pursuit Track.

name: Titan Training.
rule: 1. **Train.** The Titan Training *Train* technology ability can only be used to add a Training or Conditioning pattern card to a Titan, not a Strain.

name: Titan Genetic Memory.
rule: 1. **Genesium.** The Titan Genetic Memory *Genesium* technology ability can only be used to add a Strain pattern card to a Titan, not a Training or Conditioning.

name: Aristeia Inducer.
rule: 1. **Tokens and Awakening**[4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.sn94cfg9mzlo)**.** After Awakening, remove all tokens from the Aristeia Inducer.

name: Atlantean Oscillator.
rule: 1. **Full Hit/Full Miss**[2](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.yo3y8htv7ww)**.** The extra attack die from this weapon is included when checking if Full Miss or Full Hit abilities trigger.

name: Context.
rule: 1. **Variable Weapon Trait**[5](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.cfsnnm125tv2)**.** The Trait change ability on this card lasts until the end of your attack.

name: Cyclopean Hand Mirror.
rule:
1. **Removed Obol.** If the Obol drawn at the start of battle from this card’s ability has been removed from the Obol deck when you would need to draw it, you still draw it.

2. **Start of Battle Draw**[4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.sn94cfg9mzlo)**.** The Start of Battle ability on Cyclopean Hand Mirror is *not* considered an Obol Draw.

3. **Modified Draw**[4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.sn94cfg9mzlo)**.** When you draw the predestined Obol from the Start of Battle ability on Cyclopean Hand Mirror, you may use abilities like Second Chance to do a standard redraw.

name: Hekaton Panoply Armor.
rule: 1. **Crit Evade**[9](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.63fkdh4as5zy)**.** The Crit Evade ability on Hekaton Panoply Armor should say “Attack” instead of “AI card”.

name: Knuckleshield.
rule: 1. **Crit Evade**[9](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.63fkdh4as5zy)**.** The Crit Evade ability on Knuckleshield should say “Attack” instead of “AI card”.

name: Time Booster.
rule: 1. **Improved Rush**[9](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.63fkdh4as5zy)**.** The Improved Rush ability on Time Booster should have a Complicated Action as an additional cost.

name: MWE-4 "Keelbow".
rule: 1. **Daze**[9](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.63fkdh4as5zy)**.** The Daze keyword on MWE-4 "Keelbow" should be: “Daze, but ignore the adjacency requirement.”

name: Alpha Temenos.
rule: 1. **Maze Reconfiguration.** Ignore written text, treat it as saying “When Temenos is instructed to place a Labyrinth tile, if you can follow C.1.1.4 (a and/or b) but C.1.1.1 fails, each Titan that would’ve had a Labyrinth tile placed under them instead draws an Obol.”

name: Chimera Metastasios.
rule: 1. **Movement.** *Chimera Metastasios* movement is considered Pushback X, where X is the *Chimera*’s Speed value, but only to Titans that either were adjacent to it at the start of the Attack Action or were the Target of the Attack. All other Titans treat *Chimera*’s movement as normal.

name: Dahaka.
rule:
1. **Fearful Hospitality.**

1. **Precise and Passing**[2](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.yo3y8htv7ww)**.** The Fearful Hospitality card grants the Precise keyword to the Titan that has it.

name: Hekaton.
rule: 1. **Friendly Warning.** *Hekaton’s* Friendly Warning Trait is still used for Level 1 Hekaton. Remember that it does not affect Unavoidable Knockback ([VI.3.1.1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.842mk8bofqxf)).

name: Hermesian Pursuer.
rule: 1. **Flare Card Timing**[4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.sn94cfg9mzlo)**.** When resolving *Hermesian Pursuer’s* Flare cards, if it says “Perform Signature”, resolve it after the current action. Otherwise, if it says “Perform Heal”, resolve it after the Wound step. In all other cases, resolve it immediately.

name: Bellerophonic.
rule:
2. **Bleeding.**

1. **Ignoring Buildup**[4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.sn94cfg9mzlo)**.** If you ever get to “Ignore Bleeding Buildup”, skip all of the start-of-turn effects on the Bleeding Condition card.

2. **Ability Ownership.** Any Titan may use the Stop Bleeding Other ability as long as any Titan has the Bleeding Condition.

name: Covetous/Greed-crazed.
rule: 4. **Death.**

name: Fear/Dread.
rule: 6. **Hatred/Frenzy.**

name: Knockdown.
rule:
1. **Stand Up Effects.** When an effect instructs you to “stand up”, discard the Knockdown condition card.

2. **Ability Ownership.** Knockdown only prevents a Titan from using abilities owned ([I.2.13](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.gjrc53z0r2ft)) by it.

name: Muffled/Sealed.
rule: 9. **Paralyzed.**

name: Terror/Holy Terror.
rule: 1. **Persistent.** This condition card is not discarded at the end of battle.

name: Time-Clocked.
rule: Time-clocked follows all of the rules and clarifications of Knockdown, except that you also cannot be targeted by anything.

name: Wishkeen/Wishagog.
rule: 8. ## **Terrain Tile Clarifications** {#terrain-tile-clarifications}

name: Endless Staircase Entrance.
rule: 1. **Moving While Occupied**[2](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.yo3y8htv7ww)**.** When the Endless Staircase Entrance tile moves, a Titan standing on it stays on the origin space (i.e. does not move with the tile) and suffers any effects of leaving that tile/entering that space.

name: Gash.
rule: 1. **One Draw.** See [VI.9.6.4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.y85ji9ck2clm).

name: Giant Gash.
rule: 2. **One Draw.**  See [VI.9.6.4](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.y85ji9ck2clm).

name: Hermes.
rule: 1. **Speed Modifier**[11](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.6qjqvg8no8bj)**.** The Hermes Godform Speed modifier can be applied before or after any other Speed modifiers.

name: Labyrinth X.
rule:
When a Primordial is instructed to resolve “Labyrinth X”, do the following, in order:

1. **Step 1: Attempt to Place Tile.** Place a Labyrinth tile of the shape shown by the Labyrinth track on the Primordial sheet such that at least one space of the Labyrinth tile is both adjacent and in front of the Primordial[3](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.yutlao695syn), and that no spaces are under the Primordial. If this is not possible, skip placing the tile. If X is greater than 1, you cannot place the tile such that it invalidates the placement of another tile, unless there is no other way to place it.

   2. **Step 2: Move Marker.** Regardless of what happens in Step 1, move the Labyrinth marker clockwise on the Labyrinth Track one space.

3. **Step 3: Repeat.** If X is greater than 1, resolve steps 1-2 again in order until you have done so X times.

   4. **Additional Placement Rules.**

   1. **Under Target.** If possible, always prioritize placing the tile under the Target if Labyrinth X was triggered by an Attack card.

   2. **Under the Most Titans.** Always attempt to place the tile under the most Titans possible without breaking any of the other placement rules.

name: Strangers.
rule: No inherent effects.

name: Captives.
rule: For each Captive you have, lose 1 Crew limit. You may lose 1 Captive when resolving a Spartan Stronghold ([VIII.3.1.4](#bookmark=id.seny1d30k7ek)) to ignore that resolution.

name: Combo.
rule:
A Primordial with the Combo mechanic adds the following effects to the end of each AI card resolution: “If this is the first AI card resolved this round: Window of Opportunity. Combo”.

1. **Resolving Combo.** When instructed to resolve Combo, resolve [X.3.2](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.5psxrehb98g9).

2. **Broken Combo.** If a Combo is broken ([A.20](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.23gyftlor75d)), do not resolve the Combo.

   1. **Breaking a Combo.** All battles with the Combo ability add the following ability to all Titans *(the ability is written on the Primordial sheet, but is owned by the Titan using it)*: As a reaction you may break the Combo by gaining the Priority Target and exhausting a weapon that matches the current space on the Combo Track if you are within that weapon’s effect range and you are not currently the Priority Target.

3. **Combo Track.** During setup, place a Combo token on this track, as instructed. Whenever a Combo is performed or broken ([A.20](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.23gyftlor75d)), move the token to the next space on the track, clockwise.

1. **Track Spaces.** Each Combo Track space has a list of weapon traits used when resolving Combo Breaker ([A.20](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.23gyftlor75d)).

4. **Ignore Highest Rage.** While the Combo mechanic is in play, ignore [X.5.2](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=id.86qbana3nl9f).

name: Crippled Titans.
rule: For all purposes, when a Titan is Crippled, treat it as if it died (as if you no longer have it), except you do not remove it from the Argo (mark it as Crippled in the Stoa section of the Argo sheet). *(Crippled Titans can be un-crippled with the Trauma Growth Technology card.)*

name: Defectors.
rule: Defectors are a resource. You may spend 1 Defector when rolling a Diplomacy Test to add \+3 to the result. You cannot gain Defectors until you research the XXX Technology card.

name: Prisoners.
rule: When you reveal a BP card with the “Prisoners\!” flag, you may decide to voluntarily fail the attack before performing the Power Roll ([X.5.7](#bookmark=id.bk8zg89nthce)). If you do, resolve the Fail Response, as normal.

name: Refugees.
rule: For each Refugee you have, lose 1 Crew limit.

name: Spartan Dreamwalkers.
rule: All Dreamwalker-class Titans acquired in Cycle 2 (even from campaign setup) come with the Spartan Dreamwalker strain Pattern card.

name: The Strong Eat.
rule:
In Battle against Primordials with this Trait, add 1 additional “You died.” Obol to the Obol deck.

1. **Ignoring.** If a Titan is  instructed to ignore this Trait, remove the added Obol card when that Titan is about to draw an Obol.

name: Backwards Momentum.
rule:
In Battles with the *Backwards Momentum* Trait, during setup, assign each Titan a pair of Time Anchors tokens of the same color. They keep one, and place the other as indicated on Battle Scenario diagrams.

1. **Reestablish Time Anchor.** At the start of a Titan’s turn, it must reestablish its Time Anchor. To do so, move its Time Anchor to its current space. If a Titan is on a VP, place their Time Anchor adjacent to the Primordial instead.

name: Death by Fate.
rule:
Primordials with the *Death by Fate* Trait can deal Fate in addition to Danger with their Attacks. When a Titan is dealt Fate, still resolve [VI.9.3](#bookmark=id.f8yotdpymxzx), but use Fate instead of Danger and do not resolve the Armor Roll. Being dealt Fate over 9 does not cause you to draw a Moiros card (ignore [III.3.3.2](#bookmark=id.pjoudbvkqtm6)).

1. **Trauma Draw.** When instructed to resolve a Trauma Draw with *Death by Fate* in play, select the higher of your Fate and Danger, then compare that to your Trauma Table.

name: Delphian Dreamwalkers.
rule: All Dreamwalker-class Titans acquired in Cycle 3 (even from campaign setup) come with the Delphian Dreamwalker strain Pattern card.

name: Fragmented Landscape.
rule: Not all sections of the Cycle 3 map are connected. If you travel to a tile that isn’t connected to the rest, set aside a new portion of the map. If you ever find the tiles that link them together, do so.

name: Map Symbols.
rule:
1. **Time Wound.** Time Wounds can be moved through. To do so, set aside the map tile noted next to the Time Wound, then move the Argo to the Time Wound tile. When you do so, skip the Exploration and Expedition steps of that Campaign Day. During the next Campaign Day, you must move the Argo to the set aside tile.

   1. **Adversaries.** Adversaries treat Time Wound tiles as adjacent. Adversaries cannot move onto the Time Wound tile.

   2. **Time Portals.** If you have the required Technology, you can move through Time Portals. When you do, do not connect the tile you go to the tile you came from.

   1. **Adversaries.** Adversaries treat Time Portals as adjacent. Take Time Portals into account when considering the shortest path to the Argo during the Adversary Movement.

name: Paradox.
rule: No inherent effect.

name: Timefront.
rule: Some Battles use the Timefront Terrain. Refer to the specific Battle Setup or Primordial Sheet for details.

name: Babelian Debt.
rule: Babelian Debt is a Cycle 4-specific Argo stat with no inherent effect.

name: Curse Costs.
rule: Some gear cards in Cycle 4 cost a Curse Affliction to craft. In order to craft that gear, an Argonaut with the noted Affliction loses it to craft that gear. Gear with Affliction costs cannot be crafted if no Argonaut has the indicated Affliction.

name: Cursed Traits.
rule: Cursed Traits are Trait cards that are added to Battles when instructed. To add a Cursed Trait, shuffle all the Cursed Trait cards and draw one at Random. Cursed Traits are returned to the Cursed Trait deck after each Battle they are used in.

name: Dahaka AI/BP Deck.
rule:
The Dahaka has a unique singular deck of cards representing both its AI and BP cards simultaneously. For all purposes, treat the top half of the card as an AI card, and the bottom half as a BP card. Ignore the other half while referring to one or the other. For the BP card, use the AT value on the right of the AT symbol.

1. **Interrupt WoO.** There is a special Window of Opportunity symbol between the AI and BP portions of the Dahaka’s combined AI/BP deck. It functions as a normal WoO for all purposes, but also allows the use of Interrupt Attacks. All Titans in the Dahaka Battle gain the following ability: while adjacent to the Dahaka during this WoO, you may initiate an Interrupt Attack if you do not have a Combat Fatigue *(this ability is written on the Primordial sheet, but is owned by the Titan using it)*.

2. **Interrupt Attacks.** Interrupt Attacks are a type of Titan Attack (all Bonus Attacks are considered Interrupt Attacks). They are resolved as normal, except…

   1. …you do not draw a new BP card. Use the bottom half of the Interrupted AI card instead. *(Thus, if the AI card does not have a bottom half with a BP card, an Interrupt Attack has no significance.)*

   2. …if you hit, effects that trigger during or before the Draw a BP card step of a Titan Attack trigger immediately.

   3. …you use the AT value on the left of the AT symbol.

   4. …you resolve Interrupt responses instead of the regular responses.

   5. …if it is not a Bonus Attack, gain a Combat Fatigue token.

   6. …cause a Chain Break.

name: Doomed Investments.
rule: Doom cards in Cycle 4 allow you to invest your Progress tokens to gain various rewards. The Doom cards give you instructions on how many you need and what rewards you gain from them.

name: Endless Staircase.
rule:
The Endless Staircase is a set of rules and tiles that are part of some Battles.

1. **Tiers.** The Endless Staircase can consist of up to 4 tiers. When you are instructed to construct a Staircase with X tiers, use the X tiles with the smallest numbers. No matter the number of tiers, the Staircase is considered one continuous thing.

2. **Entering.** A Titan enters the endless staircase when instructed, or by being on the Endless Staircase Entrance Terrain tile at any point on their turn[5](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.cfsnnm125tv2). *(You cannot enter the staircase this way outside of your turn. If you start your turn on the tile, immediately enter*[5](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u3q2ngOWhVvixLbz3PiR-4HG0A1OpcAzCM-9cveigu4/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.cfsnnm125tv2)*.)*

   1. **First Steps.** Whenever you enter the Endless Staircase, unless instructed otherwise, always place your Titan on the ‘1’ space.

   3. **Movement Action While Inside.** While inside the Endless Staircase, a Titan cannot use its Movement Action to perform a Titan Movement.

4. **Combat Action While Inside.** While inside the Endless Staircase, a Titan can use its Combat Action as normal, or it may use it to perform an Ascent action.

5. **AT Values While Inside.** While inside the Endless Staircase, some BP cards will have different AT values. If there is more than one listed AT value on a BP card in a Battle that uses the Endless Staircase, use the AT value on the right while inside the Staircase and the value on the left while not inside.

6. **Ascent.** To perform an Ascent, draw any number of Major Trauma cards. When you choose to stop drawing, perform a full standard Titan Attack. After finishing the Attack completely, add any Ascent Bonus to your Power Total. Then, perform an **Ascent Check**: if your Total Power is greater than or equal to the sum of the numbers on all Trauma cards you drew, move that number of spaces up the Endless Staircase and place all Trauma cards you drew for this action on the bottom of the respective deck. If it is less, shuffle all Trauma cards you drew for this action into their deck and do not move.

   1. **Hermes Move.** After succeeding an Ascent Check, if you have any unexhausted gear cards with Hermes Move X, you may exhaust one of them to move X additional spaces.

   2. **Against the Babelian Lunacy.** While Battling against the Babelian Lunacy, if you successfully perform an Ascent Check, you may instead Wish Away to the nearest empty Irem Tower Terrain tile.

7. **Moving Beyond the Top.** If you would move beyond the last step of the Staircase, immediately Wish Away to any Irem Tower Terrain tile on the Board (if Battling the Demidjinn) or move to any VP and gain the *Lander* Condition card (if Battling the Babelian Lunacy).

8. **No Space Limits.**  Multiple Titans can occupy the same space of the Endless Staircase.

9. **Adjacency.**  Titans within the Endless Staircase are considered adjacent to the Primordial for all purposes.

10. **Targeting.**  Titans within the Endless Staircase can still be Targeted.

11. **Involuntary Movement While Inside.** If a Titan has to perform involuntary movement of any kind while within the Endless Staircase, move that Titan down that many steps instead. A Titan cannot move lower than the ‘1’ space.

name: Infinite Timeline.
rule: There is no end to the Timeline in Cycle 4\. If you run out of days to mark, keep playing, but resolve a Timeline Battle every day. These Timeline Battles are always against Level 6 Primordials.

name: Oases.
rule: During Cycle 4, there are extra Cities referred to as Oases. An Oasis is not a City for the purposes of other effects, nor vice versa, but Oases have all rules of Cities, including a separate Last Visited Oasis token that functions identically to the Last Visited City token, except it applies Oases instead. Oasis Negotiation abilities are the only Negotiation abilities that can be used at Oases.

name: Persian Dreamwalkers.
rule: All Dreamwalker-class Titans acquired in Cycle 4 (even from campaign setup) come with the Persian Dreamwalker strain Pattern card.

name: Reap (X).
rule: When instructed to Reap (X), mark an event on the Timeline with the same name, 5 days from now. On that day, you lose X if X is beneficial, or gain X if X is detrimental. Then, make a Reap Save (6+). If you fail, repeat this process. Whenever you choose to mark a Reap (X) on the Timeline, also make a Reap Mark.

name: Sow (X).
rule: When instructed to Sow (X), mark an event on the Timeline with the same name, 5 days from now. On that day, you gain X if X is beneficial, or lose X if X is detrimental. Then, make a Sow Save (9+). If you succeed, repeat this process. Whenever you choose to mark a Sow (X) on the Timeline, also make a Sow Mark.

name: Ten Thousand Nights and Days.
rule: The Ten Thousand Nights and Days is a set of adventures with a strict linear order. Whenever you resolve one of these adventures, you must go on the next one listed on the track on the Argo Sheet, from left to right.

name: Wished Battles.
rule: In Cycle 4, you may gain the opportunity to participate in a Wished Battle. These are imaginary Battles against a specific Primordial for the sole purpose of training. You gain no consequences whatsoever from Wished Battles, and you return to your campaign as if it never happened (there must be no changes from before to after the Wished Battle).

name: Wished Gear.
rule:
Wished gear is gear that gives you temporary access to Mnestis Theatre gear.

   1. **Nodes.** When you gain a Wished Gear, note it on your Argonaut sheet. Each time you Battle (even if you don’t use a Wished Gear), mark a node. Once you have marked 3 nodes, the gear disappears.

   1. **Just a Little More.** You may gain 2 Argo Fate when about to mark the third node to ignore that mark.

   2. **Just a Taste.** When you equip a Wished Gear, immediately gain the *Wishkeen* Condition.

name: AE-Siren.
rule:
The AE-Siren is an experimental Small Primordial.

1. **Spawning.** Usually, you will be told to spawn the AE-Siren from adventures. When this happens, place it on the nearest tile with a Silver Remnant and without the Nemesis. If there is no such tile, note its name on the next Timeline Battle day.

2. **Map Effects.** If the AE-Siren is on the map, at the beginning of the Campaign Round, make an Argo Fate save roll. If you roll equal or higher than your current Argo Fate, nothing happens. Otherwise, if the Argo is adjacent to the tile with AE-Siren, it must move directly away from the AE-Siren’s tile this day. If it is not adjacent, it must move towards the AE-Siren’s tile via the shortest path.

   1. **Resisting.** If you fail the save roll, you may gain X Argo Fate to succeed instead. X is the difference between your roll and current Argo Fate.

   2. **Moving Onto.** If the Argo moves onto the AE-Siren’s tile, remove it and see the *Siren Hunt* Special Event.

   3. **Nemesis and Adversary.** If the Nemesis or Adversary are adjacent to the AE-Siren’s tile when they would move, they move onto the AE-Siren’s tile instead, ignoring other Adverary Movement rules.

3. **In Battle.** At the start of a Battle with the AE-Siren, trigger its Spawn effect. Then, at the beginning of each Primordial Round, before the regular Primordial resolves any effects, resolve the AE-Siren’s AI *(Lure & Curse)*. The AE-Siren can be attacked just like a Primordial, using the listed To-Hit value, AT value, and Responses.

name: Aligned with Nemesis.
rule: Cycle 5 uses the term “Aligned with Nemesis” instead of “At War” for diplomacy. Treat “Aligned with Nemesis” as “At War” for all purposes.

name: Cycladian Dreamwalkers.
rule: All Dreamwalker-class Titans acquired in Cycle 5 (even from campaign setup) come with the Cycladian Dreamwalker strain Pattern card.

name: Disappearing Primordials.
rule: When a Primordial disappears, its miniature is removed from the Battle Board and it is now considered to be “outside the Battle Board”. A Primordial outside of the Battle Board cannot be targeted.

name: Double-Sided Map.
rule:
The map tiles in Cycle 5 are double-sided, possessing a surface side and an underwater side. While on the surface side, the Argo is considered Surfaced, and while on the underwater side, the Argo is considered Submerged. Even though there are two sides to the tiles, it is still considered one tile for all purposes, and you may only resolve icons on the side you are on.

1. **Transitioning.** When flipping a tile from one side to the other, all game elements are moved to the other side.

   2. **Movement.** The Argo can travel to the underwater side of map tiles by submerging.

   1. **Submerging.** The Argo can become Submerged during the Movement Step by gaining \+1 Argo Fate, or by losing 1 Hull when the Nemesis is about to enter the Argo’s tile. When this happens, do not move the Nemesis onto the Argo’s tile.

   2. **Surfacing.** The Argo can become Surfaced for free during the Movement Step. You cannot surface if there is no surface side of the current tile. If you would be forced to surface and cannot, lose 1 Hull instead.

name: Intermediary.
rule:
In Cycle 5, one Argonaut will be the Intermediary. They gain 1 Rage at the start of all battles.

1. **Passing.** The Intermediary can be passed to a different Argonaut voluntarily during the Timeline Step. If the current holder dies, immediately pass it to another chosen Argonaut (including the new one).

name: Mnestis-Generated Gear.
rule: Mestis-generated Gear are temporary Gear cards gained in a variety of ways in Cycle 5\. Whenever you gain a Mnestis-generated Gear, draw a random one from those available (Secret Deck 18, cards 299-303). All Mnestis-generated Gear is archived after completing a Battle.

name: Nemesis.
rule:
The Nemesis is an evil Argo. It follows movement and spawn rules of Adversaries, but is not considered an Adversary for other effects.

1. **Evaded.** The Nemesis does not move while the Argo is Submerged.

2. **Resurfacer.** When the Nemesis moves onto a submerged tile, flip it to the surface side.

3. **City Breaker.** If the Nemesis moves through or onto a City, place a Ruins token there. If the Last Visited City token is there, gain 1 Doom. *(Because of C.5.7.2, this means this will only ever affect surface Cities.)*

4. **Endbringer.** If the Argo ever occupies the same map tiles as the Nemesis, you lose.

5. **Relentless.** At the end of the Exploration step, move the Nemesis towards the Argo as instructed by the current Doom Card.

name: Oxygen and Aether.
rule:
Oxygen/Aether tokens are double-sided tokens (one side is Oxygen, the other is Aether). They are used in Battles with the *Oxygen and Aether* Trait (all Battles in Cycle V), in which each Titan starts with two tokens on the Oxygen side.

1. **Oxygen.** At the start of a Titan’s turn where this Trait is in play, they must flip an Oxygen token over to its Aether side. If a Titan ever needs to flip or discard an Oxygen token and cannot, they die.

2. **Aether.** Each Aether token that a Titan has gives that Titan a \+1 to Awakening rolls. When a Titan Awakens, that Titan discards all Aether tokens.

name: Paranoia.
rule: Paranoia is a Cycle 5-specific Argo stat with no inherent effect.

name: Ruins.
rule: If a map tile has a Ruins token on it, ignore any City icons there. When a Ruins is placed, remove the Last Visit City token from that tile.

name: Sermons on the Shoals.
rule: Sermons on the Shoals are Cycle 5 special adventures. They follow the same rules as Ten Thousand Nights and Days from Cycle 4\.

name: Silver Remnants.
rule: During Cycle 5, there are extra Cities referred to as Silver Remnants. A Silver Remnant is not a City for the purposes of other effects, nor vice versa, but Silver Remnants have all rules of Cities, including a separate Silver Remnant token that functions identically to the Last Visited City token, except it applies to Silver Remnants instead. Only Silver Remnant Negotiation abilities are the only Negotiation abilities that can be used at Silver Remnants.

name: Smother.
rule: All Battles in Cycle 5 use the *Smother* Trait.

name: Titan Facing.
rule:
In Cycle 5, Titans use Facing Indicators to mark their current facing. Determine ‘in front’, ‘rear’, etc. the same way you would for Primordials.

   1. **Changing Facing.** Titans may start facing a chosen direction at the start of Battle, and may change facing at the end of a Movement Action or move-like ability, as a free action, or when performing a Bonus Attack.

name: Titan X and Adversary Activations.
rule:
Titan X interacts differently with Adversary Activations than other Adversaries. It has its own 7-space track that sits off to the side of the map.

   1. **Spawning.** When you would spawn Titan X, place the Titan X token on the ‘7’ space of the Titan X track instead.

   2. **Moving.** When you would move Titan X, move the Titan X token 1 space down the Titan X track instead. If it would move off the bottom of the track (below the ‘1’ space), trigger an Adversary Battle. If you don’t move the Titan X token during an exploration step, move it up 1 space, ignoring C.5.15.3; it cannot move beyond the top (‘7’) space.

   3. **Red Spaces.** When the Titan X token moves onto either of the ‘3’ or ‘6’ spaces of the Titan X track, place the Titan X voyage miniature on the map, on a random unexplored tile adjacent to the Argo, or any adjacent tile if there are no adjacent unexplored tiles.

   4. **Voyage Miniature.** The Titan X voyage miniature does not ever move, it is only placed by C.5.15.3. However, triggering an Adversary Activation while on a tile with the miniature still triggers an Adversary Battle.

name: Trireme Abilities.
rule: In Cycle 5, all Trireme Argo Abilities from previous Cycles are disabled, except for the Titan X battle where they are all enabled. Cycle 2 Trireme Argo Abilities used this way function with Cycle 4 Trireme rules instead of Cycle 2 Trireme rules[14](#bookmark=kix.vncfldnua34g).

name: The Truth Machine.
rule: 19. **Underwater Cities.** During Cycle 5, there are extra Cities referred to as Underwater Cities. An Underwater Cities is considered a City for all purposes, except it has a separate Underwater City token that functions identically to the Last Visited City token, except it applies to Underwater Cities instead. Some effects apply to Underwater Cities specifically, and not to regular Cities.

name: Starting a New Cycle.
rule: 1. **Titans.** At the beginning of each Cycle, if continuing a campaign, you will be told a number of Titans you get to start with. Choose up to that number of Titans out of the ones you ended the previous Cycle with to keep and lose the rest. They retain all properties they had at the end of the previous Cycle. If you ended the previous cycle with fewer Titans than the indicated number, first gain one of each Argo-bred class you have the technology for but don’t already have, then gain Dreamwalker-class Titans until you reach the required number.
